title: Issue-lifecycle process definition + cascade
description: Formal shape of workflow.yaml at schema_version 3 — the issue lifecycle rebound onto the shared process substrate (COR-031) as a KEYED process (COR-032), per DEC-032. The substrate-shaped fields live under `process` (id, version, subject, states with `detection` predicates, transitions with optional `gate` predicates); the pm-LOCAL blocks (`cascade`, `closure_triggers`) and pm-local fields on states/transitions stay top-level / inline and are ignored by the engine. Companion per COR-018. The five states (todo, backlog, in-progress, review, done) keep their meaning; state ORDER under `process.states` is load-bearing (the engine returns the first matching detection, and forward cascade infers 'ancestor is behind' from the canonical ordering).

process.description: The substrate-bound process definition (COR-031 shape). Conforms to ../../../schemas/_defs/process.schema.json#/$defs/process; restated here with pm's domain-specific field shapes for data↔companion validation (COR-018).

version.description: Definition version (handles definition changes under live subjects).

subject.description: The issue lifecycle is keyed: many issues, each at its own position (COR-032).

cardinality.description: keyed (COR-032): the engine operates per a supplied issue number; it never enumerates.

key.description: Descriptive: names what identifies a unit (issue-number).

states.description: States, ordered so the engine's first-matching-detection rule and the forward-cascade ordering both hold. Order is load-bearing.

transitions.description: Allowed transitions. A transition not listed here is forbidden.

cascade.description: Pm-LOCAL (DEC-032 D3; engine ignores). The three cascade behaviours. Forward and closure cascade upward through ancestors; downward cascade is explicitly disallowed.

closure_triggers.description: Pm-LOCAL (DEC-032 D3; engine ignores). Recognised paths by which an issue reaches the Done state.

run.description: Name of a command the capability registers in package.yaml (the engine rejects an unregistered name).

predicate.description: A checkable predicate the engine runs as a read-only subprocess (COR-031).

mode.description: Position inferred from reality via the predicate (COR-031 core).

detection.description: How the engine resolves whether an issue is in this state.

kind.description: deterministic: the predicate's result decides (e.g. checkbox close-gate). authorisation-artifact: the predicate returns {exists, produced_by} and the ENGINE computes result = exists && produced_by != actor (e.g. PR-merge cross-authority).

gate.description: What must hold for a move (COR-031 P4; always reduces to a checkable predicate).

display_name.description: Display form used in board UIs and prose (e.g., `Todo`, `In Progress`, `Done`). Pm-local; engine ignores.

meaning.description: Load-bearing (the status view renders from it): what an issue in this state represents.

applies_to.description: Pm-local. Issue-type tokens for which this state is reachable. Review is task-only; the other four apply to every issue type.

entry.description: Marks a start state (COR-031). Todo is the unconditional entry.
Per-state shape. `detection` is the substrate-shaped position predicate; `display_name`/`applies_to` are pm-local.

to.description: Target state id. Equal to `from` for PR sub-lifecycle rows (no issue-state change).

trigger.description: The named action that drives the move (COR-031). Matches `command` for pm.

command.description: Pm-local. The verb-subject workflow-wrapper command that owns this transition (DEC-026).

pr_state_effect.description: Pm-local. The PR-state effect the owning command drives.

authorisation.description: Who may move (COR-031). `user` = user holds the gate; `agent-autonomous` = agent flips without prompting.

executor.description: Pm-local. Which actor performs the mutation.

applies_to.description: Pm-local. Issue-type tokens for which this transition is valid.

severity.description: Pm-local. Severity the agent applies when a user-gated transition is attempted without authorisation.

why.description: Status-view prose: why this move exists (COR-031).

hint.description: Status-view prose: what to run next (COR-031).

notes.description: Optional prose. Edge cases, examples, integration-branch variants.

transition.description: One allowed transition. The substrate-shaped fields are from/to/trigger/authorisation/gate/why/hint; command/pr_state_effect/executor/applies_to/severity are pm-local.

behaviour.description: Prose describing the agent's action when a triggering condition is met.

applies_to_levels.description: Issue-type tokens the cascade walks through as ancestors / descendants. Empty array means the cascade is disabled.

idempotent.description: Whether re-firing the cascade on already-cascaded ancestors is a no-op. Forward cascade: true.

requires_user_authorisation.description: Whether the cascade prompts the user before mutating each level. Closure cascade: true (per-level).

cascade_rule.description: One of the three cascade behaviours.

target_state.description: Final state the trigger drives the issue to.

applies_to.description: Issue-type tokens the trigger applies to.
